how_it_works: Build archetypes describe the fundamental scaling strategy of a character. Most builds fit one primary archetype, though hybrids exist. Understanding your archetype determines which stats to prioritize on gear.
tips: Attack builds need to check accuracy rating — Resolute Technique eliminates this but locks out crits.
Spell builds ignore accuracy but must convert or scale added damage type correctly.
DoT builds care only about the first hit's damage for scaling — not the number of hits.

archetypes.name: Attack
defensive_layers: Armour or Evasion (Strength or Dexterity base)
archetypes.how_it_works: Deals damage through weapon hits. Scales weapon damage, attack speed, and added damage. Accuracy must exceed enemy evasion or you miss — aim for 90%+ chance to hit. Crits are optional but powerful: needs Crit Chance and Crit Multiplier investment.
archetypes.notes: Most beginner-friendly archetype. Gear drops (weapon damage) directly translate to power. Melee attacks benefit from Fortify for reliable damage reduction.

archetypes.name: Spell
archetypes.how_it_works: Deals damage through spells — no accuracy needed. Scales % spell damage, % elemental damage, and specific skill modifiers. Spell builds often go critical strike for large damage multipliers. Intelligence base gear supports Energy Shield life pool.
archetypes.notes: No accuracy requirement makes gearing simpler. Wand + shield or two-hand staff are typical weapon setups. Many Witch and Templar Ascendancies support spell builds.

archetypes.name: Minion
defensive_layers: Life (Necromancer typically runs max-block or Bone Armour)
archetypes.how_it_works: Your character summons and commands minions that deal damage on your behalf. Your own hit damage is irrelevant — only minion-specific stats matter. Minions inherit auras. The player focuses on survivability and buffing minions rather than dealing damage directly.
archetypes.notes: Player survivability is paramount — if you die, all minions die. Raise Spectre builds require knowing which Spectres to raise (Redemption Sentinels, Solar Guards, etc.). Necromancer Ascendancy is almost mandatory.

archetypes.name: Damage over Time (DoT)
primary_stats: Hit chance (only matters for applying the DoT)
defensive_layers: Chaos resistance (Poison scales off chaos damage)
archetypes.how_it_works: Deals damage through a persistent effect (DoT) applied by a hit. The hit itself may deal little damage — only the DoT matters. DoT builds focus on maximizing damage over time multipliers and dot-specific stats. They are often immune to reflected damage since DoTs cannot be reflected.
example_skills: Ignite: Burning Arrow, Explosive Arrow, Scorching Ray
Poison: Poisonous Concoction, Cobra Lash, Toxic Rain
archetypes.notes: Extremely safe against elemental reflect. Poison builds use Chaos damage scaling. Ignite builds often use Elementalist or Chieftain. Bleed builds use Gladiator. DoT is not reduced by armour or evasion of monsters.

archetypes.name: Trigger / Cast on X
primary_stats: Attack speed (affects trigger rate in CoC builds)
Critical strike (required for Cast on Crit)
archetypes.how_it_works: Uses a trigger mechanic to automatically cast spells on every hit (Cast on Critical Strike), when taking a hit (Cast when Damage Taken), or on kill (Cast on Kill). These builds link an attack or spell to trigger a second spell automatically. Cooldown recovery rate controls how often spells fire.
example_skills: Cast on Critical Strike + Glacial Cascade/Exsanguinate
Cyclone + Cast on Crit + Ice Nova
Cast When Damage Taken + Immortal Call
archetypes.notes: Cooldown recovery rate is a critical stat — often needs 152% from gear or Awakened gems. Mechanically complex but extremely powerful when optimized. Inquisitor (Inevitable Judgement) is the most common Ascendancy.

archetypes.name: Aura Support
archetypes.how_it_works: Dedicates the character entirely to running as many powerful auras as possible for a party. An Aurabot can run 10+ auras simultaneously, massively buffing party damage and defence. Solo viability is limited — this archetype is almost exclusively a party/duo role.
archetypes.notes: Requires very expensive gear to reserve multiple auras efficiently. Almost exclusively played in group content. Guardian Ascendancy is the classic Aurabot class. Solo, Aurabot has mediocre damage but excellent survivability.

archetypes.name: Hit-based Elemental
archetypes.how_it_works: A spell or attack build that deals elemental (Fire/Cold/Lightning) damage and scales elemental penetration to bypass monster resistances. Often applies ailments as secondary effects (Shock for more damage taken, Freeze for safety, Chill for slow). Elementalist is the premier class.
archetypes.notes: Elemental Equilibrium (EE) keystone is common in elemental builds — hitting with one element reduces resistance to the other two. Elementalist Ascendancy provides unmatched elemental bonuses.
